title: Exodus: Oppression and Deliverer
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of exodus: oppression and deliverer and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study exodus: oppression and deliverer and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.
Review question: What was the name of Moses' mother who hid him for three months? Answer: Jochebed.
acceptable_variants: the levite woman who hid him

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: The title of Egypt's king; the Pharaoh of the exodus is not named in Scripture but used all royal power to resist God's demand to release Israel
Egypt's great river; the setting for Moses' infancy as Pharaoh ordered Hebrew boys drowned in it, and the site of the first plague when God turned it to blood
